reviews.review_text: Really like this caravan park. We stayed in a tent and the campsite was a good size. Our friends stayed in the cabins and they were really well presented and set up. They had two bedrooms, one with a King bed and one with 4 beds (2 bunkbeds). They have a great setup for children having a pool, waterpark, play area and bouncy thing, along with a games room so perfect for the kids. Worth checking out. Really good staff, and good facilities for all people.
Great park great service enjoyed our stay there
Loved the Glamping Tent.  Large KS bed was so comfortable, tent was well appointed and the amenities around the tents was fabulous.
we have a C/van and love staying here love there auctions take advantage of these @ every opportunity the pk is in a great position that you can walk into town

What a fantastic park! Full of kid friendly fun & let’s not forget the adults!! absolutely love the pavilion and the bar cart - this was our first time staying at this park and it won’t be the last. Thankyou so much.
Have been here many times and such a great park. I wanted to mainly shout out to Kristen who works in reception who as on a few occasions now, been so kind and helpful over the phone!
There's something special about watching a movie snuggled up with your kids in a bean bag under the stars. Honestly such a fun park to stay at, thank you.

Great location, nice park, albeit a little dusty where we stayed but that was dirt sites not lawn so expected but the neighbours roosters need to go! From 3am every morning they were relentless.
Pool was nice, good entertainment with the footy on the big screen!

Internet one star
our room tbh $400 pn for 5 of us on par with others but we wanted  a park fam friendly got what we wanted

Wifi not up to par.

Great camp ground. Has a nice pool and water park. Close to Tanunda main street and wineries. Went on the long weekend. Park had movies for the kids,  pop corn vendor, different food options each night and a coffee van.
We had a great site for our caravan for our visit. Amenities were good and clean. There is a water park for kids. It is aneasy walk into town.

Staff were friendly but the park smelt like 💩 due to some "stagnant grey water overflow" which stunk out our camper, feral cats everywhere at night in the bins and anything thats not put away, poor t.v, internet and phone reception and the neighbour on the hill has filled his yard with Roosters which crowed from 10:30pm until daylight and kept us all awake. Kids loved the pool area so that was nice and it's also a short walk to town. I wouldn't stay again just because of the neighbours roosters, it was nuts.
Lovely caravan park close to centre of town with great amenities.
Excellent caravan park, such friendly staff, very relaxed environment, great activities for the children. Amenities are spotless and in great condition. We will be back!
Great location.  Caravan Sites are a reasonable size.  Amenities could do with an update to match area.  Staff very nice and welcoming. Pool was great.

Not one to leave a review but are stay was terrible the photos on the website are very deceiving. And to find are cabin in such bad condition ants everywhere and the smell of bold. Also to have someone else set up a tent 6inchs from are cabin is just ridiculous My babys food and anything we didn't put back in the car over night was covered in ants. Left first thing in the morning. Would have been more comfortable camping instead of paying $220
The room was clean, and as described on rhe website. School holidays was busy but the park coped well. Amenities were also clean.
Great central location to access all that the Barrossa has to offer

Loved my time away the Barossa is a beautiful area, staff were very welcoming I had the privilege of talking with the assistant manager (Tiff)  who explained to me that they run all different sorts of activity’s throughout school holidays and long weekends
She was very welcoming, kind and helpful she met all my needs 10/10 would recommend
This Caravan park is fantastic, So many fun activities for the kids, great location & very friendly people. Will definitely be returning
Thsnkyou for your friendly hospitality.

A very nice clean caravan park.
Enjoyed our stay there.  Thank you.
We stayed on a powered site, we loved it! Very well run and looked after. Polite staff! We will definitely return. 🤩
Location second to none, a short drive to everything…  staff were absolutely lovely upon check in!!! Amenities clean and well maintained during peak periods

Fantastic for a quiet family getaway. We stayed in a cabin which worked perfectly. Best mattresses I've ever slept on at caravan park. We enjoyed the evening and morning cockatoo visits, though a little noisy. The park is beautifully located and provides many great family activities.

I’m not usually one to leave a review however felt it was important to share our experience with the deluxe 3 bedroom house. Travelling for the first time with 3 toddlers we wanted to make sure the kids had a safe place to run around away from cars and in a secure environment. When we came across the 3 bedroom house on the parks website we were instantly drawn to the large backyard with lovely green lawn shown in the photographs of the house. However, when we arrived we were allocated to a house with a tiny outdoor area covered in sharp rocks. There was no disclaimer on the website that the pictures shown may be different to what would be provided. Had we known, we would have opted to stay in a cabin and saved ourselves the additional $600.

We also discovered that the doors leading to the outdoor area had only sliding locks and could not be deadbolted or locked with a key, meaning that the kids were able to get outside and access the road through a gate which also did not lock.

We were visited by a variety of spiders and insects throughout each room of the house, the furniture and blinds were very dusty and the kitchen sink also had to be plunged during our 3 night stay.

The pool and the splash park were fantastic. However we were unfortunately very disappointed with the accommodation where we ultimately spent most of our time.

We have been three years in a row in the first week of January. The facilities are great. The toilets and showers are always clean. The pool and water park are a god send in the heat and keep the kids entertained.

Great campsite, perfect for families with kids.
Super place for children, very close to the city. My daughter was especially happy because she got to see little kittens at the campsite. A lovely, friendly atmosphere – we would definitely come back!

Probably one of the worst cabins i have stayed in. Each window had black mould around the window frames. The cabinetry was falling apart. Curtains barely held together. Do not book a budget cabin if you value your families health. Time to do better and carry out basic maintenance also two shower heads in the closest toilet block were broken. Given it’s such a busy holiday period you would think they would stay on top of this. Looks absolutely nothing like the online photos will not be back. False advertising at its finest.
Very disappointed.one staff member was rude.cleaners rude.lost so much money cause i couldnt stay due to heat.pool was dirty

A great campground for exploring the Barossa Valley. We stayed three nights on an ensuite site. The bathroom was the best we had at the entire trip around Australia– other campgrounds should take note. Shower gel, shampoo, conditioner, body lotion, and towels were all provided. The children had a lot of fun on the playground. There are movie nights, a water playground, and a swimming pool. The campl kitchen and barbecue area are very well equipped. The only downside was that the pitch itself was a bit small. We only had a 7-meter-long camper trailer, and I wonder how a larger motorhome would fit. Perhaps there are larger pitches available.

My Wife and I stayed in Superior 2 Bedroom Access Cabin - Sleeps 5 for 3 Nights

Excellent Location close to places people want to see

Staff friendly easy to access all amenities
Great family caravan park and very popular with families, great vibe at Halloween
We didn't stay overnight, only visited people there, they have everything to entertain the children and great kitchens.
Fabulous park and great fun for the kids for Halloween 💙

Great place. We stayed in the safari tents and they were amazing. Love the private parking and pool. Don’t going during a storm, it’s very loud with the wind and rain flapping on the tent. Also loved the water park even as an adult. So much fun.

Unfortunately the powered caravan site was shorter than the overall length of caravan and car due to refurbishment to the park. As I had booked the site well before the work had begun I was a bit miffed at having to unhitch on an angle so I could detach the van and then tow the drawbar across the site to straighten the van up. There was total lack of concern shown by the staff when made aware of the predicate. The park was not fully booked so there were other sites available.
The place is amazing,  that why we've been coming for 10 years or more!

My compliments to Lea at the front desk, she was very accomodating and informative and accommodated my special requests. as well as the other 2 ladies at the front desks what a bunch of lovely ladies. The toilets and showers are big  and clean. The sites are very easy to access with our motorhome and  a few minutes walk through the social hub of Tanunda.

This was our first visit to Barossa Discovery parks but it won't be our last. Dog friendly cabins, lots of green space and less than 1hour from Adelaide. We had a comfortable stay. And my dog enjoyed meeting lots of new people and furry friends. My cabin was clean, comfortable and dog friendly.

Park is fine....reason for mid range rating:
Lousy tv (none) reception, parks wifi also doesn't extend away from rec room area.
Staff agreed with me on Lousy tv reception said they were aware of it....blamed the parks location. Don't recommend staying here..(ofcourse if you have starlink, above doesn't matter)
Perfect location to start a wine tour, everything is walking distance..plenty activities for the kids.
Good location for Tanunda...amenities great

Lovely park, good location, have stayed here before, close to town. Stayed in deluxe 1 bedroom cabin, bedroom wasn't very clean, dust on side cupboards and bedhead, could see its been a little while since dusted. Skirting dusty to. Lounge and bathroom were clean.

We have stayed at this par a few times and have enjoyed our stays. Great facilities for children so good for family stays.
It is walking distance to town if you fancy a meal out or just some shopping.
Lovely cabin with great facilities, in a lovely quiet park.
A great base to go and explore this fantastic wine region.Very clean and tidy amenities and although the park was full it was quiet and relaxing. Highly recommend 👌

Its such a beautiful an peaceful place ❤️an to sit out on the porch an have some crackers an dip or dinner an a couple of drinks an hear the birds an kookaburras it’s just so beautiful an water park for the kids an school holidays is a good way for kids to make friends an memories an the staff put on some fun things for the kids an they love the movie nights aswell an the food an drinks vans that come on the school holidays are cool aswell ❤️
We had a lovely stay in one of the lodges. The pool was heated and the weather was warm enough to enjoy it in August

Enjoyed my stay here. Staff were very friendly. Cabin was great. So close to Tanunda. Thanks for the Beaut time we had.  If you could check the heating in the dryers, as it seems to not dry properly (thermostat!). Many 😊 Thanks.
Great.
Great cabin 👌 nice spot, great for wine lovers travelling through

Stayed in the ‘glamping tent area’. The tents were lovely and well fitted out with quality bedding.  The spa at the end of the large pool was greatly appreciated after our 30km walk that day.  The people that worked there were warm and accommodating.  I’d definitely go back for a longer stay with a book and bathers.  A great central location for exploring The Barossa.
It was absolute tranquility so peaceful and relaxing such beauty the cabins are clean and well maintained with everything you need children and pet friendly can't wait to go back

Stayed for 5 days as they had a special of $20 per night.  Was put at the back of the caravan park were it had been raining.  Very muddy and boggy. Said that we did not want to stay on that site but they did not care.  Had plenty of powered sites in the modern side of the park but would not allocate us one.  No WiFi unless you stayed in the office.  Could not access WiFi on the powered sites at all.  Would not recommend to stay on the old sites in the park and won't stay there again.  Would rather go free camping instead.

Great place to stay, heaps of fun for kids, with the water park and pool.
Great showers. Come visit you'll love it.

When we arrived we were advised there was no linen in the cabin, so when one of the workers asked another if she could do it she just sighed and said no, we were then just given a huge bag of linen to do yourself. Black mould in the cabin as well as ants.

We were very disappointed, having stayed here in the past our expectations were pretty high. We were after a site that wasn't surrounded by trees because of our Starlink, we were given a site and then told if it didn't suit find another one and just let them know. The first site didn't suit so we found another one, when we let the office know we were told we couldn't have that one. Everyone we tried and selected we were told we couldn't have it so in the end we cancelled our booking and moved onto Big4 in Nuriootpa which is a much nicer park and the service was so much better.

Had an absolute amazing time recently stayed in the safari tents for a few nights. The serenity was so lovely, calm and quiet plus having a dedicated pool and hot spa was beautiful. Staff were really helpful when needed and great location. Will definitely be coming back.

What a fantastic site.  We stayed for two nights and really enjoyed it.  Just a short walk to the town and the supermarket/bottle-o.|Our two bed cabin was so well equipped with everything we needed, and the heat pump was a godsend.

:

⸻

🌟 A Wonderful Stay in a Cozy Cabin – Discovery Parks, Barossa Valley
We had such a lovely getaway at Discovery Parks – Barossa Valley. We stayed in a cabin, and it was super clean, cozy, and well-equipped with everything we needed. The space was peaceful and perfect for a relaxing break.

The park is beautifully maintained, and the staff were very friendly and welcoming. We especially enjoyed the outdoor areas and the pool — everything felt calm and inviting. 🌳

I took some great photos because the surroundings were just stunning! This place is definitely on our list for future holidays. Thank you for the warm hospitality! 💛

Highly recommended for anyone looking for comfort, nature, and a peaceful escape.🥹🥰🥰
Beautiful spot!

Great park with clean amenities and friendly staff. Fantastic park for families in particular with a kids movie night and playpark plus great swimming pools!!!
Would recommend this park.

We stayed for 5 nights at Discovery Parks Barossa Valley. The park is well maintained with clean and modern facilities. While premium sites are available, we were allocated what seemed to be an older part of the park, still tidy and functional, with awning mats provided, but the site felt a little cramped for our 9m van, which was a tight fit. This may have been due to the promotion we booked under, so something to keep in mind when selecting site options.
Overall, the park is lovely, peaceful, and offers everything you need for a comfortable stay. Its location is ideal as a base to explore the beautiful Barossa region and local attractions. Would definitely recommend, just be mindful to confirm site size if travelling with a larger van.

We favour free or hip camps, but this time we wanted to be in the heart of the most well known of Australia's wine growing regions.|In year's past this park had a reputation of being a bit rowdy. But our experience was the exact opposite! We had a beautiful unpowered site close to amenities  which were new and clean and to the fully outfitted camp kitchen.|We stayed 2 nights and loved it.

Stayed here a couple of times. Caravanning and also in the rooms.
The pool is amazing and I spent majority of the day in there. We got a park right in front of the pool.
Another time I went  the room I stayed in was immaculate and so clean. Would definitely stay again. I got the superior 2x bedroom room.
Very kid friendly.

We stayed in the glamping accommodation, and apart from the very noisy group who took over the whole enclosed seating area at night and at breakfast time Saturday morning, we enjoyed our stay.  We will be checking before we book next time as that really detracted from our enjoyment Friday and Saturday night.

Have sent on line enquiries to this park and they never answer. Do yourself a favour and go to Nuriootpa.ADDING FUTHER INFORMATION. MUM AND DAD HAVE MADE SEVERAL PHONE CALLS TO THIS PLACE. ALWAYS A RECORDED MESSAGE AND NO ONE HAS BOTHERED TO RETURN THEIR CALLS.
Barking dogs, lots of barking dogs.  Would be a great park if they delt with the barking dogs morning and night 🤷‍♂️. Did I mention the dogs in the bathroom, laundry and BBQ areas.

This is a great place to stay if you’re visiting the Barossa Valley region in SA. We have a caravan and have stayed several times. The deluxe powered sites are excellent and other powered sites are very good. Some have awning mats that are excellent but others have none. The camp kitchen is very good and staff are extremely friendly. In warm weather the pool great and kids love the water park. Overall it is a very good place to stay and a nice walk into town.

Beautifully clean park, well situated and comfy accommodation.
Big shout out to the beautiful girls at reception. Leah, Britt, Julia and Emily. All very friendly, welcoming, respectful and good to have a banter with. See you all next time  Deexx
Great clean tidy park with excellent facilities and fire pit. Location perfect for touring the Barossa. Only downer was the slowest pizza oven this side of the country.
We were in our own accommodation,

Immaculate grounds, spotlessly clean top class facilities and every staff member we spoke with was extremely helpful and friendly. In my opinion one of SA top sites - no rabbits running around and digging holes either!

Affordable get away.
Two bedroom cabins are spacious,  especially if you're on your own. 😆
Has everything in the cabin, including soap, hand lotion, dishwashing detergent, microwave, iron, relaxing sofa, nice outside patio setting, etc
营地条件不错 有很棒的封闭厨房 户外还有BBQ区域 卫生间也很干净，就是营位不是特别大，但也很不错 营位上有草地和地垫

We dropped in to make the booking a month prior and chose cabin with 2 rooms for us both. We hadn't received a confirmation email/text so had to ring and confirm before we arrived.  One room room did not have the bed made up   with the sheets and doona on the end. With slight mobility issues it was difficult to make the bed up.

Awesome,clean ,accessible.
5 minutes to town .
Pretty town

Great van park but DO NOT get site 114. The galahs above poo all over the site and your van overnight. This a very well run park which would be excellent for people with kids, it has everything they would want
Very beautiful tree filled setting. Huge outdoor movies everynight. Great bike trails too. Friendly staff. And clean amenities

What an incredible park. After a friendly and seamless check in we set up camp in our ensuite caravan site. Ensuite hut was immaculately presently in a generous grassed site. The park is beautifully maintained with a plethora of services to enhance every holiday makers experience. This park is holiday heaven for families with fun for kids front of mind. That said we were travelling without kids and the park was peaceful with the soundtrack of happy kids in the background. Nightly movies and football games were shown on a large screen on a lawned area with reasonably priced quality food trucks and bar in attendance. Alternatively if self cater isn't available to you the camp kitchen is extremely well equipped including pizza oven and is right next to a great games room and communal space. All a short walk to the centre of town. We can't wait to return.
Great roomy caravan sites. Excellent for kids. Lovely clean park close to town

The beds literally looked like it had bedbugs. The room smelt like pure cigarette every inch including the couch, bunk beds and the master bedroom. There were stains on the sheets. There was little droppings on the top bunk bed.The AC wasn't even working. The good thing was the waterparl which was fun for the kids but overall not a great place for families to sleep at.
Ask for movies every night, they love people asking about what movie is on! They love to let the kids choose :)

For me, it's not somewhere I would want to go to again, but it's ok if you're a wine buff. Not that close to other places if you're thinking of going on foot, plenty of wineries to drive to, catch a taxi or take bus tours to. I believe the park is mainly for families, with the now standard water park, pool and jumping pillow a feature. Apart from that, not much. I must add that the facilities/amenities are very well maintained and the staff are extremely friendly.

A recent return, not because I wanted to, but because family staying long term, that were charged at a very inflated price compared to other grounds not far away.
Too many negatives to put it all down here. My last review I said I probably won't be back, and this has confirmed my thoughts, don't care who is there. Much rather go to the one at Nuriootpa, where we are now.
Great park close to town

Bit of a novelty here - we stayed for two nights in a glamping tent! It was basically set up like a cabin, with a bathroom and kitchen attached. We enjoyed the comfortable bed and good water pressure in the shower. Not to mention eating breakfast on our small verandah in the morning sunshine. Only downside is the cold. In early April the nights were already below 10 degrees, and being in a tent, it got cold very quickly. The heating worked well to overcome some of this, but it was hard getting out of bed in the morning. Also, there's no TV - didn't bother us, but might bother others. Regardless, I would stay again as I loved the experience.

]]7NEVER AGAIN. Visited from Victoria for famed SA AFL Gather Round. Paid $375.00 per night for a very average cabin. I came to the Barossa to watch football - both live at Lyndoch and then hoping to do same in cabin - is advertised with TV etc etc. Terrestrial TV reception was shocking - and Channel 7 - which is what the other Gather Round games were televised on was totally unwatchable - retuned TV and tried everything - nada! Tried streaming Kayo via a) hotspotting my phone and b) the CP's "Free" Wifi - which had no bandwidth whatsoever and dropped out roughly every 30 secs - which I had to log into again and again - GAVE UP. SO - I ask management, considering the exorbitant amount of money they have charged me for 3-nights accommodation (in a cabin which was still dirty, bedroom door kicked in and shocking water pressure et al) how can they remedy the TV/WiFi issues - and do you know what I got told. A) The terrestrial TV antenna was 7kms away and the CP being in a "hollow" means the park suffered with FTA TV delivery - WOW! Don't advertise that you can provide TV then - get the super wealthy Discovery Parks label to put up their own park booster!!! B) Stream content via CP free WIFI (bahahahaha) or hotspot your own phone - same problem as A. Goodness - we're 1 hour from a major capital city and you would get truly better IT support and infrastructure in a 3rd world country - True!. Thanks Discovery Parks for wrecking my long weekend - I came for the football - couldn't go to every match of course - so relied on being able to watch in my "Superior Cabin" that's prices were jacked-up massively for the privilege. NEVER AGAIN DICOVERY PARKS - NEVER AGAIN - and not just Tanunda but ALL of your parks. Get with the game and provide the infrastructure require to meet your advertised inclusions - AND please don't tell me again that because the van park was near capacity that everyone was trying for a piece of the crappy bandwidth - put boosters in or moreover get a better ISP !!!!!!!!!
Paid over $100 per night for a powered site. The site was just dirt and dust. No grass. No concrete, no ground cover, just dirt!!

Van onsite. What a great little park in the Barossa . Amenities clean and tidy serviced every day . Plenty for Children to do in park in a safe secure environment . Walking distance to town centre.  Staff are exceptional polite and try hard to meet your needs. Highly recommend

Perfect retreat away in the heart of the Barossa!
Staying in the glamping tents is the perfect retreat with relaxing pool and exclusive outdoor dining space.
Park is clean and tidy. Staff very helpful as well.
Very friendly stuff, clean well maintained camp - highly recommended 👍 - walkable distance to Tanunda wine bars.

We spent 5 nights here on a caravan site and discovered Tanunda and all the surrounding towns.  This Park is excellent, great amenities block, cheap ($4) washing machines and fabulous water park and outdoor evening movies for kids.

We had a grass site except for the dog crap at the back of our caravan. It was like a mine field, had to watch where you walked.
The other 2 complaints is the TV reception and mobile reception. Could have done with a booster for both
Cheers John
Great and well equiped campground. The un powered area can get very full. Wifi is good but unstable.
Fantastic place for children a beautiful place to stay you can't go wrong
Was a glamping tent holiday " very modern and nice " /sadly no shade on the balcony till after 6pm /no TV "
This is a nice place for families with small children. We stayed at a small budget cabin, which was good but not great. But there are plenty of larger cabins that look very nice.

Stayed in standard 2 br cabin. Well appointed with necessities. Cabin needed maintenance outside as wooden railing on verandah were collapsing. Wine glass in cupboard was dirty. Bedside table was dusty. Floor was sticky. Bathroom vanity had water damage.

The pool is great on the hot days. The bathrooms are maintained well. The trees are great for shade but the sites are a struggle for people in huge caravans. I got delayed leaving because people couldn't vacate their sites once the dite opposite them was full

An excellent base to explore the Barossa with Tanunda main centre just walkable away.||The unpowered site is very cute, tucked away next to facilities and nicely maintained.  It is not marked out so arrive early to get a nice setting as it is very small area.||We had a lovely 3 days - used this as a base and also did an organised wine tasting tour with Experience SA who kindly picked us up /dropped us off at the entrance.

Couple of nights stay for a family occasion, really good facilities, but parking spaces a bit small.  Why do these big parks cram us all in.  I don't need to hear next doors conversations all night.  Real shame.
The best packed sardine tin around.

A really great and well kept campsite. There were lots of great amenities and everything was kept clean and well looked after.
We stayed in an en-suite powered site and it was perfect, it was clean and modern and the extra towels, shampoo & other bits make it very comfortable.
Campers are respectful and quiet and we had no issues at all during our stay
Great family Holiday
Great spot will come back

Love this park.  We stayed in one of the delux cabins.  Bed was so comfy.  The only negative are the kitchen chairs.  The lounge isn't big enough for the group we had so some sit outside or on the kitchen chair, but they're all metal.  Not nice to sit on.  Especially when we were sitting around the table to play cards.  Please change the kitchen chairs.

I visited a family here who were camping for a couple of nights. The water activities looked great. Plenty of kids and adults taking advantage of the facilities. Small water slides for young children. A bouncy mound for the kids. Free BBQs plus an air-conditioned room with plenty of tables (to eat one's meal at).  Fridge and freezer available for use. Kettle available for boiling water. Dishwashing liquid plus tea towels available. Possibly the road noise of the night could be an issue. Certainly was for the first night. All up I was impressed with the quietness of the park.

My husband and I stayed in a Safari tent for the first time. The room was nice, except there was no TV and you can’t park the car near the tent.  While the area is for 18+ year olds, which obviously attracts older people, it’s NOT disability friendly at all.  With the walkways being river bed pebbles, we struggled to wheel our cases over it and there aren’t any handrails on the steps up into the tent. We liked the accommodation, location, the friendly staff and the adults only pool but have decided not to stay in that type of accommodation again due to us both having disabilities.

Einfach nur furchtbar viel zu teuer,  zu laut weil es direkt an der Hauptstraße liegt.  Die Kabine konnte man nicht abschließen weil das Schloss kaputt ist,  der Fernseher ließ sehr zu wünschen übrig weil die Sender durch Pixel immer mehr verschwanden . Das Licht ging ebenfalls nicht weil die Lampe kaputt war  . Es staubt auf dem ganzen Platz,  Wäsche waschen war tabu deswegen und der Rest sah auch nicht anders aus  . 150 Dollar pro Nacht  =  ein Wucher  !  Nie mehr  . ...

If I could give it 10 stars I would. We booked 5 nights in a superior two bedroom cabin with our two kids and 5 nights turned in to 6 nights as we didn’t want to leave! Beautiful park, relaxing but always something to do. Hubby and I loved our morning coffee from the coffee van on-site, the kids looked forward to 7pm movie nights, they lived in the pool and the water splash park was amazing! We cannot wait to come back. Thank you so much for a beautiful 6 nights away 😊

Always a pleasure staying here in the heart of the Barossa. Tried glamping here - its on par with others just a bit of a hike to your tent if u have one of the back ones but private pool and cooking area - a little piece of quiet.
We stayed on a powered site. The amenities were always clean. Loved that there were daily activities for the kids including movie nights and a colour run as well as different food trucks each day.

Yet another example of a National Holiday Park Group taking advantage of occasional holiday families. To explain we made a booking in 2021 and paid a $440 deposit but due to unforeseen circumstances were not able to make our weekend event planned. This deposit was put into credit to be used at a later date and at that time there was no advice regarding using the deposit within 12 months. We subsequently booked to attend the same annual event in the area in January 2024 and were led to believe our deposit would be valid for that stay. Unfortunately our family was struck down with covid and once again we could not use the paid deposit. Now in 2025 we are healthy and wanting to attend the same event only to find our deposit will no longer be honoured and we are expected to pay the full rate for a two night stay in a 3 bedroom house. This will make our attendance at the event ridiculously expensive so we have angrily opted to lose our paid $440 deposit and not visit the Barossa Valley region at all. Simply not good enough and an absolute ripoff. Hope they have enjoyed using our $440 for their coffers.

Awesome that this place is dog friendly. Amenities were nice and clean.
However as a rooftop camper we would not recommend the unpowered site section as it a main thoroughfare for pedestrian access to the amenities block. As well as kids on their bikes riding up and down the gully that surrounds this site.
Overall this is an overpriced site at $72/night.

This place is amazing. They really have worked out how to cater to family's. The night movies with food trucks were fantastic. The facilities were great and the staff there are fabulous. I thought our last stay at a discovery park was great but this park is next level great. Well done to the team there.

Very busy. Premium site was big & clean. Toilet block very clean. Loved the food vans. Staff lovely.  Recommend staying here. Dog friendly - just be a responsible owner and don’t leave your dog tied to a bike for 2 hours unattended.

Great little spot. Water park and pool for hot days was great. Showers and toilets nicely cleaned. One area I wasn't happy a bout was the spider webs built up in the camp kitchen and the men's toilet block. Invest in a cob Web broom or brush and I would of given top stars 🌟

Loved our stay here! There were food trucks, live music, activities for the kids during the day, and coffee in the morning! It is very family friendly, they have great amenities, close to a lot of the wineries that Barossa valley has, and just a short walk your on the Main Street.  I would 100% recommend staying here.

Staff very friendly, helpful, polite and awesome camp site was fantastic ensuite was very awesome and wonderful I would recommend coming back here again the pools is great and other activities are fantastic.

We live in a bus so we had a powered site. All the facilities here are fantastic, beautiful location obviously being in the barossa and my step son had the best time we could barely get him out of the pool!
Well situated. The heated pool was great. The dust is annoying.

In town for a wedding, location easy and convenient. Gentle stroll into town. Love the coffee van on site. Water park and pool for kids.
Cabin good size, everything needed including the ironing board/iron.

We loved it here. Lots to do for the kids with movies at 19:00. Play rooms and great BBQ areas.
Clean and well maintained rest rooms.
Sites were spacious.
Have only been here a day but the facilities seem nice and all very clean, staff are friendly and plenty for the kids to do.
We attended the water park/pool and bbq area and found them to be clean and well maintained, including the toilet facilities. Friendly staff. We are looking at visiting again in the new year.
So many bugs in the cabin. They was dirty tissues in the drawer and roaches in the sheets!

We celebrated my husband's 40th birthday, and our son's 9th birthday at the discovery park, it was lots of fun! We loved the superior cabin, the aircon worked well and we enjoyed the outdoor setting with family. The pool, water park and outdoor cinema were fantastic 👌

Absolutely terrible...we were given an allocation of a grassed site. Sounds nice well it wasn't grass. Just loose dusty dirt. In 38° heat it was very uncomfortable. Vans jammed in like sardines. We were so close to the people behind our awnings almost touched. The price for this dust bowl was the same as a grassed site. Now come on guys if you can't deliver what you advertise then drop the price or at least tell us.
Been on the road 8months, worst park so far for the price. This park seems to be repeat offender going by the reviews. Do better people, stop with the excuses and do something about it.
Romantic setting and a well lit place with plenty to do. Came for our honeymoon and was surprised by the spacious room available in the Safari Tents.
Everything was perfect!
We stayed in our caravan, this park would have to be in the Top 10 in South Australia!!

This was our worst stay in a caravan park..at Tanunda discovery park. Bathroom miles away. Spot ground so uneven. Couldnt use pool cos too many paying to come in use it and water world. Was way crowded for that was a plus for paying in parks use!  First person at desk was serving us just got a radio message and left... had to wait for other worker. Asked for a ensuite site and said wasnt any 2 were vacant for our entire stay! Never again. Bought membership so hope Discovery other parks are a good site better...literally.

Our ‘dust bowl’ site 92 was a metre from a new construction site of small cabins.
That was somewhat tolerable until some worker starts up a saw at 645 am. 🥴
Me not usually one to complain let it go thinking he’s just not thinking!
Next morning at 740 am a massive truck arrives and proceeds to drop topsoil 20 metres from the back of our van. Noisy enough but the dust etc went everywhere.
We were booked in for another night but packed up and moved on.
On departure we let the office know and ‘Harry’ boy wonder in the office was not sympathetic at all. He turned it around to put the problem on us. Why didn’t you complain ? Here’s the number to call ! At 645 am what were they going to do ! Why were we placed near a construction site for 4 nights when the park wasn’t full?
We never asked for a refund and of course he never offered. He bordered on being an arrogant 30 yr old with a bad attitude with zero sympathy.
Barossa was beautiful as was the small town of Tanunda but never again to the Discovery Park
Lovely park, we were in the unpowered section. Which was a nice green area near amenities. Lots of sun & shade. Great showers.

Was pretty let down. Wanted an up market quiet weekend. So choose the glamtenting. Was anything but that. Park had a band playing so loud you could hear it in town.  This was a planned event which wasn't told to customers. Well not me anyway.  Then those staying in glamping stayed on there porch till 1 am very noisy. Now the tent paint pealing, toilet sticking and had to be punched, hard bed and pillows. And was it boring no facilities for music , TV or even books. They tell you grape vine views  I didn't see any.  But before all this they over charged me almost $50. That took alot of argument to get back. Think before you stay at this park its far from cheap and should be much better looked after. I've stayed at some places that needed repair but didn't pay this sort of money to stay there. Regular price is around $200 a night
Stayed on a powered site. Great caravan park and facilities.  Pool was even heated somewhat which was a bonus. Waterpark is like ice though.

Excellent place to stay. Has so much to do for both adults and children. Family oriented. Extremely friendly staff who are very helpful. Other guest are also very friendly making for a lovely atmosphere and pleasant stay.
Great park for families, heaps of playground to keep them busy.. Great for anyone...
We enjoyed our stay in the park. Kids loved the swimming pool and water park section.
Great park for families, great location.
Great place to go with the kids and the pool is heated which is perfect after you've been in the water park. Keep up the great work team.
Without a doubt the best park I've ever stayed at facility wise. 10/10

Overall a very clean CP.   However, our experience was very disappointing.  We were placed “up the back” in an overpacked area, and a very tight site to get into.  The trees also made it literally impossible to drive our van out, which meant the only way to exit our site at the end of our stay was back my van out.  Honestly, ridiculous! Had we have known all this, and that we’d be assigned a site right under the very noisy Barossa Valley Way, and on a very slopey site, we would have paid the extra dollars and stayed at the Big4 ten minutes on in Nurioopta.  If you decide to stay at the Discovery Park Tanunda then ask to stay in the flat, clear, peaceful sites at the front, and not in the sites up the back near the permanents.

Lovely caravan sites and with good amenities
Outdoor barbecue facilities and nice camp kitchen
There is a stage presumably for entertainment purposes
There is a pool and a water park

Very impressed by the park and facilities.

We stayed in a safari tent while visiting for the weekend.

Clean, well maintained. Also loved the fact the safari tent area was adult only which also had its own pool, jacuzzi, covered kitchen/BBQ area and fire pit.

The park has all the boxes ticked with great facilities for both families and couples.

Grounds and public areas were all clean and maintained.

The park team were helpful, polite and friendly.

Site was quite after dark. Only a 10 minute walk in to Tanunda via a secure back gate.

A credit to the discovery park brand!

🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟
Everything was good, clean, and well maintained. Camp kitchen lacked utensils and crockery. We were on an uncovered site so no comment about rooms.
Very good caravan park, will back again.

We stayed lastnight in 1 of the pet friendly 4 person cabins and it was lovely.
Extremely clean and the park is beautiful.
The pool, water park and playground areas look like so much fun.
Pity we couldn't stay longer.

We will be definitely booking another trip for a few nights soon.
Thank you for a lovely stay ❤️
Great

Called in to inspect the park today 28th July at 10.30am we are  2 couples both who own caravans in our 70s
1 person is  disabled and was REFUSED entry  to look around. we were told to Park car and walk around park not drive.
We were disappointed not able to look around..

The cabins are not very clean. Staff are rude and no customer service. No communication. Very noisy children running around late at night around cabins. Cabins need updating, and better service with towels, etc.
Great facilities for children at this park and a large camp kitchen
Great caravan park
Close to town and plenty of opportunities to explore The Barossa.

Great staff, great location, plenty of cafe's, eateries, pubs and wineries nearby.
Also, great little gift shops to look in and buy locally made gifts, jams and sauces. The chocolate factory has sooo much to choose from!!
Great local op shops too 😊
The cabins are fantastic. Very clean, with everything you want/need, the showers to die for!! Very reasonably priced as well.
We had a pet friendly cabin, there's plenty of space to take the dogs for a walk.
A very quiet location too.
All in all, a great time was had, can't wait to come back!!

We stayed in the safari tents and they were amazing. The park staff have catered for every additional need you may have - including a Nespresso pod machine, utensils for the BBQ, and a well stocked fire pit. 10/10 would come again.
Tent was warm and cosy even on a cold June night.
The wider park is amazing for families, with plenty to keep the kids entertained (jumping pillow, playground, water park).
Tanunda was a great location to explore Seppeltsfield
There was so much planned for families over the long weekend in June. We booked only a few weeks ago and there was plenty of space prior to the weekend.
Great park with clean and modern facilities. Park managers are amazing and friendly. Recommended park to stay at if in Barossa.
I didn't give it 5 stars because of the grumpy old guy driving around having a go about kids playing , they're kids for Christ sake !!
We stopped here travelling back to Perth, the sites for the caravan were nice grass the bathrooms were clean and well maintained, and the Jacobs Creek vineyard is just around the cornre.

Was a really nice 2 days away staying twin cabin
Close to town  go AC but electric blanket would have been better  as 4 C this morning

We stayed 3 nights in a Glamping tent, and thoroughly enjoyed the experience. So quiet and peaceful, and waking up to the birds in the trees was wonderful.
The tent was so roomy, and the shower was magnificent. The best we've had in years of motel and cabin stays. The staff were so friendly and helpful, always with a smile!!
We'll definitely stay again when the need arrives.

Service was frustrating and lacking. As we were arriving late, we called several times before we heard back. When we finally spoke to them, we were told we would recieve a text detailing check-in process, this never happened. When we arrived and finally spoke to a gentlemen, who was actually quite helpful, thankfully, we had to find our way around the park to our accommodation. When we did find our parking spot, we had to walk a reasonable distance, which initially was quite in an unlight area. Would not have liked to have been an senior citizen. We also tried to book another night and were told we would need to be moved as others had booked. They also seem inflexible with booking arrangements.

Location great , and rooms clean and fine.
Facilities good but absolutely appalling customer service by Rhiannon on duty. The rudest person I have ever come across. Not a good advertisement for your business at all. Would not go back because of this. Laura

My fiancee and I just spent 4 nights in the Glamping tents here with friends and we loved it.
It's so nice to be in a private area of the park with no kids allowed. The tents are beautifully done with a very comfy king size bed, lovely ensuite bathroom and kitchenette. The communal BBQ and lounge area was excellent and well set out.
We are looking to forward to returning again and are recommending it to our friends.
Grand kids had ball in the water park

Stayed in April on a powered site. Had arranged for a certain site. We were assured of that site. When we got there we didn't have that site anymore. Was put on a site that only just fitted our van. It was very dusty. The ladies toilets had run out of  paper on one occasion. For the price I would have expected more.
I wouldn't stay again.

Stayed for 3 nights on one of the private ensuite sites.
Fantastic location and the kids loved the night movies on the big screen and the pool/water park.

Really bad timings kids wanted to ride on go carts and they shut at 4:30 and it doesn’t even get dark until 6

Nice location and just enough things for the kids to do
Neat well presented park - excellent for families with young kids

We had a lovely time at the park. Kids have had daily activities in the school holidays which has been great. Clean and tide bathrooms. Fantastic water park. The staff have been so friendly and helpful
It's right next to the main road, road noise is constant, but the location is good, if you are into food and wine, I won't be coming back, too noisy.
We stayed in the Glamping Tents they were very comfortable and we had a very enjoyable stay.
Great park with excellent facilities and perfect location. Kids loved it

Stayed on the 1st March and we Loved our week stay at the park. Staying at another discovery site in South Australia and the Barossa discovery was much nicer . Friendly helpful staff, walking distance to town . Differently stay again. Tom and Dian
Stayed in the safari tents and it was an awesome experience. A great little get away from Adelaide and the rest of the caravan. Sonia the lady on the desk was so sweet and helpful.

Don't get me wrong this place is absolutely gorgeous, and the pool is so peaceful. But the way me and my room mate were treated when having guests over was appalling. The guests were yelled at and told to leave for no reason and I found that it was just rude. The beds are comfortable but on my last night staying I found a bug on top of the bed sheet, also I have been constantly itchy due to the bugs. Ants have been all over the room and there is sugar on the floor in the kitchen. This place is good for the scenery but customer service and comfort is just non existent. For the price we have payed to stay here, the room would be expected to be clean, but room service and cleaning is not provided if you're staying in a room and nor is clean linen or towels. Do not recommend staying here for a long period of time unless you've come prepared.

Beautiful getaway and personal pool if you get the delux safari tents. Worth the trip for a holiday. Rooms are good for people with a disability but not in a wheelchair as there are no ramps. Maybe something for the park to consider.
Great location in the Barossa Valley. So many wineries and cafes. Family friendly park. Great pool and water Park.

Hi have made a reservation for may and wish to make the deposit BUT have noticed mistake in number of adults and have been trying repeatedly for a week to get a call back from park so I can make deposit at the right amount as very much looking forward to stay. Will someone PLEASE either answer or return my call don’t really want to have to drive all the way there just to fix a simple request thank you

So close to being so good and great fun, but lacking some refinement and quality for the high prices that they now charge and "resort" style feel.
- I really question if the pool is heated - after a very hot week it was still very cold.
-They messed up our group booking, and initially had us separated. Eventually worked something out, but shouldn't be that way. Seems it's not an isolated issue - with people who checked in shortly after us faced the same issue (and we're very angry).

It's a beautiful park and we have a great time, but really just need to lift it up a bit if they're going to charge these prices.
Direkt am Ortsrand von Tanuta gelegen. Direkter Weg in den Ort mit den Weinprobierstuben. Sanitäranlage sehr sauber. Schöne Poolanlage für Kinder

No reply to earlier email. Cabin cleaning was bare minimum. Food under the lounges and stained. Bunk beds hard to make up. Both water activities were so cold and water park too rough for 4 year old. Wasps also an issue all the way to the Cabin. Def a 1 night stay. Lovely meals about and activities cool but expensive.
Great Base for the Barossa.
Very nice holiday park with wonderful amenities.  Beautiful trees, quiet location near town.

Affordable and clean accomodation in the heart of the Barossa.

Obviously a popular location with families and retirees.

Does have problems with ants: our kitchen was swarmed within moments of putting food on the counter.
Huge park, great location. Fills up on weekends with lots of families and kids. Some sites terrible, dusty, small and no shade. Others fantastic. Choose wisely.
Excellent cabins. The park was clean and spacious. Great location. We got a great deal for our 2 night stay. Great service all around.
Great family pet friendly caravan park.
Rooms were lovely, the water park and pool were the highlight of the kids stay!
Staff are very unhelpful and rude and sites are way over priced will not be returning

We enjoyed our stayed. Very well set up. Great location. Water park and pool are fantastic 💦
Our cabin was overall comfy 🛏️
We found the lighting in the cabin poor and at night time the colour of the lights was like a hospital operating room 😳💡
Even the bedroom lamps were horribly bright. The kids bedroom didn't even have a lamp.
We suggest changing the lights to a warm light and put a lamp in the lounge.
The blinds on the windows are either down or up and when up everyone in the park can see in.
The kiosk closes at 5 on the dot. I think they should stay open a little longer in summer.
When dropping the key back the attendant seemed grumpy and uninterested, it wasn't a nice way to leave. We won't rush back here again any time soon but would still recommend it to others.

Spent the past week in the glamping tent area enjoying the smell of gumtrees, heated pool and spa, lovely BBQ gathering area. Was a perfect way to spend our 10th wedding anniversary. The tents are amazing neat tidy lovely bathroom and great shower.
No kids allowed in this area so even better! We will be back.
Fun lots to do great service pretty sites

Amazing discovery Park. All staff are friendly and welcoming. We were camping in the non-powered site the location was nice and quiet. The camping kitchen was very clean! The park is well looked after. The showers and toilet was spotless. Kids loved the pool and after a very busy day it was nice to sit back relax with a cocktail watching a movie on the big screen. We have now been to quite a few discovery parks and this one is my family favourite!
Safari 18+ tents were awesome. Second time visiting. Wish the spa was operational 24hours and a bit of shade over the pool area would be a treat. Other than that it's a great stay!

Stayed in the safari tents. Rooms are neat and clean,  amenities are great. It’s perfect for a group of family or friends with the communal area.
Staff are great and friendly. No kids policy works well.
Couple of things maybe to think about,
Hand rails at the steps to enter the tents.
Ceiling fans or evaporative air con in communal area.
The only negative experience was the Greek food truck. The yiros’s were exceptionally small and overpriced for the small size. In my opinion, the pita bread was pizza bases from supermarket. The meat was very dry and filled  with chips.
Also a smile wouldn’t go astray.
I won’t be going back.
The coffee cart was in complete contrast to the Greek truck. Coffee was good, fair price and friendly.
Loved the park will definitely be back.
Found it hard to sleep as the park was very noisy with people yelling during the night, will be finding somewhere else to stay from now on for work.
My husband and I have stayed at this park previously and were very happy with the site and staff. This time we were visiting family staying there. It was their second or third time staying there.

Nice park, but the caravan site was small and close to your neighbours, and it's like camping at a daycare centre, with a million kids about. Great if you want your young kids entertained with a water park, swimming pool, and nightly movies.  It's not my thing, but my kids are older.
Visited some friends,  nice looking park
gr8 experience in the safari tent do it again in a heart beat!
Spacious clean cabins, great location at the Barossa.
Love this park. The amenities are dated, but tidy. The sites are a good size and therr is lots for the kids to do. The pool is nicely heated too. Pet friendly.
Good
Why would you have a camp kitchen and games room (with loud gaming machines) in the same location…it’s either one or the other. It’s a ‘no’ from me!
Best amenities yet. Some cooking utensils would be good in the camp kitchen for touring cyclists. Everything is very clean. Some road and helicopter noise in the night.
So close to town. Great for kids

Stayed for the weekend in the glamping tents and have no complaints. The location, atmosphere and amenities were all great and had a positive experience. I appreciate that the glamping was for over 18s only, secluded from the rest of the park it making a relaxing holiday.
In a fantastic part of barossa at the bottom of Tanunda. Great room, comfortable with great balconies the pool and splash park are great, and coffee van, food truck and mobile bar a fantastic extra!

Had a great time here with the family.
8 year old, 5 year old, 3 year old and 5 months old. Few nights stay. Great facilities, boys had fun in the waterpark. All loved the pool, playground arcade etc.
Cabin was lovely, exactly what we needed. Can't wait to come back!!
Great weekend away with friends in the Glamping tents
Nice park.  Easy walk to main street and shops

Hallo,
ich war zu Gast im Discovery Park Tanunda und kenne ihn schon seit 26 Jahren. Ein schöner ruhiger Park am Ortseingang von Tanunda. Früher war es etwas kleiner und es gab nicht so viele Extras. Guter und freundlicher Empfang und ein guter Service. Der Platz bietet einige Bungalows und Cabins. Außerdem bietet er viel Platz für Wohnwagen und Zelte. Die Waschräume und Toiletten sind wie fast überall in Australien sehr sauber und gepflegt. Die Küche ist gut sortiert und gut ausgestattet. Der Pool, den es früher nicht gab ist schön, ebenso wie der Spielpatz für Kinder. Der Park ist sehr Familienfreundlich und einladend.
Der Park ist umgeben von viel grün und ist auch sehr schattig, was immer gut ist, vor allem im Sommer. Bis zum Ortskern sind es 5 Min. zu Fuß. Die Preise sind auch erträglich, selbst in der Peak Season. Hat mir sehr gefallen, danke.
We stayed in the Safari tents a great experience.

As a caravan park, you would be hard pressed to beat this one. It has it all, water park, pool, games room, pizza oven, bouncy pillow and so on. I have stayed at many of the discovery parks and other sites and this is one of the better ones, at least in regards to park facilities. The only main issue I had was the pool and the staff and informing the woman at the counter it wasn't heated. It was frustrating having to repeatedly explain that it was not heated and being told emphatically by staff it was heated followed by an explanation of how the heating and cooling of large bodies of water works. After being told they would investigate after several minutes of this as we all becoming increasingly frustrated, a few hours later I received a phone call explaining that it was in fact not heated on that particular day. I have stayed there once before many months prior to this and had similar issues with this pool being extremely cold. If from the moment I raised the concern it was followed by a "oh, sorry about that, I'll get someone to check the temperature" this review would have been a five and I may have wanted to revisit the park. Aside from that, great facilities and very central to Barossa and a short walk into town. Just make sure the weather is hot to take full advantage of a potentially cold pool!

There is camping and there is camping "in style" in a Safari Tent. Tents are nestled in a separate part of the park amongst gum trees and beautifully maintained gardens. There is also a kids free swimming pool. Each tent has a deck area with an outdoor setting where you can sit and relax. The accommodation itself is chic with an ensuite bathroom, a well equipped kitchenette and for those cool evenings there is a fire pit.
We really enjoyed our experience and would thoroughly recommend it...we will certainly go again.
Daniela and Charlie

We stayed for 5 nights was great except one of the on site bans had a black cat that was allowed to roam around and we had a dog and we constantly trying to calm our dog down all us people with dogs doing the right thing and this cat climbing over bbq ,s in the steps down our end should not be allowed
This  is  a  very  nice  place  to  stay  at.
Great facilities in this caravan park. Pool, waterslide and splash pad were a hit with the kids. Highly recommended

I booked a cabin at the last moment, as I’m hiking and the weather looked bad. It was a great choice - the cabin was excellent.
Very clean, with everything I needed and more. Really comfortable bed, great TV and well appointed kitchenette.
The staff were really nice on the phone when I booked, and in person when I arrived.
Great family facilities in the park, and only a short walk to the pubs and cafes of Tanunda.

The kids an I always do a quick trip getaway to come down here and just have a weekend away! So much fun, great place, great staff! Always lots of attractions, play areas and much more! Really recommend this place and we will always  ome down a few times a year! One of the best parks in SA! Hands down!

We stayed in a ensuite caravan site for our stay . They were so so close to each other it was ridiculous. Office staff had no idea what they were doing, packing in very large vans on sites they shouldn't have been on. Our annex was touching the van next to ours ,everyone was talking about how close everyone was .Very disappointing.

Excellent place, we stayed in the tents side and the toilets are very nice and clean, and well located. I saw also nice games around for the kids to play. Good and a lot of facilities. Just a bit tricky to find the spot. But very recommended for a nice camping time. I could notice that it’s pet friendly as well.
Climb the stairs and you are right in town to the right, beer garden to the left.

Fantastic location and place. We stayed  this time in cabins and they were probably the best we have stayed in within SA. Clean, beautiful canvas' on the walls, heating, and the beds are actually comfortable! Wish we could have stayed longer.

Loved our time at the Barossa discovery park. The dog friendly cabin we stayed in was great, just what we needed. It was clean, cosy and had a comfy bed. The park itself is really lovely. Looked very well maintained and had a friendly and relaxed vibe. The location of the park is excellent. It’s an easy stroll into the heart of Tanunda. We were thrilled to find a huge fenced and grassy area for the dogs to run/play about 5 mins walk from the park, as you head into town. Right beside a nice pub too - it was so beautiful. Definitely recommend this as a place to stay in the incredible Barossa.
Loved our stay here. Great place

We wanted a park close to town and wineries/eateries. Discovery Barossa ticks all the boxes. We opted for an ensuite powered site. So very glad we got an ensuite. It has to be the very best we have experienced. More like a motel, complete with fluffy towels, shampoos and all the bits and pieces. Best of all it was brand new inside and immaculately maintained.
Feels good to complement rather than complain, a nice change.
Fantastic park. And so dog friendly! Need more of these to be like this.
Clean showers and toilets, basic cabin but good value
We stayed in the campsites area and the park is well equites, clean and in a great location !!!!
Great location, we had an ensuite site for our van.  Ensuite is modern and clean, we will be back next June long weekend!

The rugby community came up and stayed for either 1 or 2 nights last weekend, and we had an absolute ball! Safe environment for the kids to run around, bouncy pillow, outdoor movie theatre for kids with beanbags, spacious communal area indoors and outdoors, plus so much more. Most of all, the staff were extremely friendly and attentive to our queries. Our cabins had plenty of amenities and kept us warm during this cold weather. Will definitely be booking again.
Friendly staff. Cabin was clean. Location was awesome. Dog friendly.
Great park very tidy with clean amenities and happy staff

We had a wonderful weekend stay at this caravan park. Our cabin was clean, comfortable and had all we needed. Lots of space around the park, a great walk along the linear path into town and back kept up my steps. Friendly staff at the check in office. Well situated for short drives to Angaston, Stockwell, Yalumba wines and many others. Thoroughly recommend a winter stay here.
Great place absolutely clean amenities with heated pool and water slides walk away from the shops and plenty of wineries 🍷

The Park is great and I particularly found the staff provided outstanding service.  I would recommend to anyone travelling through the Barossa.  The varied accommodation available is fantastic no matter what your budget or you preference.
